mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single quarter, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenues, depressing equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE breaches 5.0%, and the signal exhibits no predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, triggering risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of positive real GDP growth, an acceleration in consumer credit outstanding reflects strong household balance sheets that sustain corporate earnings, driving positive equity returns.
regime_hint: expansionary phase with positive GDPC1 growth
failure_condition: Forward SPX returns are not significantly higher when RSAFS growth accelerates during positive GDP growth periods compared to contraction periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 2.5% annualized over a three-month window, it triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not decline following CPIAUCSL breaches of the 2.5% annualized threshold in over 60% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with rising unemployment indicates a surge in labor force participation outpacing job creation, signaling weakening labor demand that precedes equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: The divergence between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE does not predict negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust business investment, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates over any 2-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.0 million units while the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5%, the combination of collapsing real estate demand and restrictive monetary policy creates a high-probability environment for equity market corrections.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where both HOUST drops below 1.0 million and FEDFUNDS stays above 1.5%, invalidating the predictive power of the joint threshold.
